781dd557-e076-42bc-9506-4c187efcd465.name: Vanilla Pumpkin Protein Cheesecake
781dd557-e076-42bc-9506-4c187efcd465.description: Base protein cheesecake (vanilla SF pudding, pumpkin for moisture/body). Launch point for flavor variants. Low-fat / high-protein; pumpkin does the work fat does in a standard cheesecake. Tuned DENSE (Tier 1+2): pumpkin reduced 120->75g + 2 tbsp AP flour for a firmer set (AP because that's what's on hand — its gluten sets denser than cornstarch), baked DRY (no water bath) to a just-set center and chilled overnight — denser at near-zero macro cost (~129 cal/slice, ratio 9.1). Stays lean; if a bake comes out rubbery rather than densely creamy, add a little fat (more cream cheese or an extra yolk).
781dd557-e076-42bc-9506-4c187efcd465.author: user

steps.instructionText: Cook the pumpkin in a nonstick pan over medium heat, 3-5 min, stirring, until thickened and nutty. Remove and COOL COMPLETELY (hot mix will scramble the eggs).
Blend the cottage cheese completely smooth (immersion blender or food processor) - no visible curd, or the cheesecake turns grainy.

Beat the light cream cheese alone until smooth, then add yogurt, blended cottage cheese, protein powder, sweetener, vanilla, lemon, and vanilla pudding mix. Sift/whisk in the AP flour smooth (no lumps) and mix just until combined — don't overwork it. Warm the bowl ~15 sec if it clumps.
Add the eggs and egg white and the cooled pumpkin. Mix just until smooth - do NOT overbeat once eggs are in (air = cracks, and we want a dense set anyway).
Pour into a parchment-lined 9-inch pan. Tap on the counter to level and pop bubbles.

Bake at 325F for ~45 min (or 350F for ~35-40 min if you want it faster) — NO water bath; a dry bake sets it denser. Pull when the center is just set with only a slight wobble (~155-160F center), not visibly jiggly. It firms further as it cools — do not chase a fully dry center, that is where a lean cheesecake turns rubbery.
Cool: oven off and door cracked 10-20 min, then counter to room temp ~1-1.5 hr (max 2 hr out), then fridge at least 4 hr — overnight is best and gives the densest, firmest set. Best the next day.

80837875-0278-4e0c-94d8-4aca7a657104.name: Crumbl Base Protein Cookie
80837875-0278-4e0c-94d8-4aca7a657104.description: The shared base cookie for the crumbl protein-cookie family — the flavor-neutral vanilla chassis every flavor derives from. Built from the tested-excellent set: Birthday Cake + Lemon dough at 1.5 scoops plus their shared cornstarch soft-crumb trick, with a lean egg white for structure. Frosting is a sub_recipe pin (defaults to the Cool-Whip vanilla protein frosting); flavors swap the pin — cream cheese for red velvet, a lemon drizzle for lemon. The 20g newcomer's heavier protein-load stays a queued experiment. Sweetener is granulated Splenda (calorically modeled, never monk fruit).
80837875-0278-4e0c-94d8-4aca7a657104.author: user

steps.instructionText: Mix the dry ingredients into a bowl — flour, vanilla protein, Splenda, baking powder, salt.
Add the wet ingredients — egg, melted butter, Greek yogurt, almond milk, vanilla — and mix to a dough. If dry, add the almond milk 1 tsp at a time until it just comes together.
Chill 20-30 min, then scoop onto a lined sheet, wet hands, and flatten (this dough barely spreads).
Bake at 350F for ~9 minutes — pull while the centers still look soft; they firm up as they cool.
Cool the cookies fully, then frost with the vanilla protein frosting (sub-recipe).

1a6ac19f-7753-4943-9fb0-0d58bbf817d9.name: Browned-Butter Protein Cookies
1a6ac19f-7753-4943-9fb0-0d58bbf817d9.description: Browned-butter protein chocolate-chip cookies, makes 3. VERSION A - Thick & Gooey (molten center): tall bakery-style mounds, intentionally uneven (gooey center, firmer edges). The thick middle cooks slowest, so the edges finish before the center - that's the price of a molten center. Soft, fragile, and pale is CORRECT; these don't brown like a normal cookie. Confirm doneness by breaking one open after resting: soft/dense/moist/baked = done; wet/gummy/raw-flour = 1-2 min more. (Version B - Even & Soft-Chewy - is a derived variant that flattens + bakes lower for one consistent texture.)
1a6ac19f-7753-4943-9fb0-0d58bbf817d9.author: user

steps.instructionText: Brown the butter: melt it in a pan over medium heat, swirling, until golden and nutty-smelling (3-4 min). Pour into a mixing bowl and let cool a couple minutes so it won't cook the egg.
Mix wet: whisk in the brown sugar, Splenda, whole egg, egg white, and vanilla until smooth and glossy. Don't whip air in - just combine.

Add dry: stir in the whey, flour, pudding mix, baking soda, and salt until a thick, scoopable dough forms, then fold in the chocolate chips. It should hold its shape, not pour. Too stiff/dry -> a splash of milk; too loose -> a bit more flour. Taste for sweetness.
Chill the dough 30 minutes.
Shape into 3 tall mounds - do NOT flatten.
Bake at 350F / 175C for 9-11 minutes. Pull when the edges are set but the center still looks slightly underdone/soft. Stays pale - don't wait for golden tops.
Rest: leave the cookies on the hot tray 10-15 min after baking - they finish cooking from residual heat and firm up from floppy to chewy as they cool. Don't judge texture until fully cooled.
Fridge (optional): for a firmer, denser, chewier bite, refrigerate the cooled cookies 20-30 min or longer. Protein cookies tighten up when cold. Store leftovers in the fridge.

5a7ddb0d-b980-4292-9771-04fd4aa98287.name: Protein Cream-Cheese Frosting
5a7ddb0d-b980-4292-9771-04fd4aa98287.description: Protein cream-cheese frosting — extracted from Red Velvet Protein Cookies as an M3 sub-recipe. Makes 1 batch (enough to frost one 5-cookie set). Its own base→variant root: Cool-Whip and other frostings derive from this.
5a7ddb0d-b980-4292-9771-04fd4aa98287.author: user

steps.instructionText: Beat the yogurt, cream cheese, protein, and Splenda smooth. Add the pudding mix to thicken, or chill 20-30 min before using.

61e41346-1e00-4685-ac72-e1f7797d2f77.name: Red Velvet Protein Cookies
61e41346-1e00-4685-ac72-e1f7797d2f77.description: Crumbl-style protein red velvet cookie with a protein cream-cheese frosting (frosting modeled inline; future sub-recipe candidate). Standalone root - base to be extracted once more Crumbl recipes land.
61e41346-1e00-4685-ac72-e1f7797d2f77.author: user

steps.instructionText: Preheat oven to 350F. Line a tray with parchment.
Whisk the wets - yogurt, pumpkin, egg, vanilla, food coloring, vinegar - then stir in the Splenda.
Add the dries - cocoa, oat flour, cornstarch, pudding, protein, baking soda, salt. Mix to a thick dough.
Rest 10 min to hydrate. If still batter-like, add oat flour 1 tbsp at a time until scoopable.
Scoop 5 cookies, flatten slightly (they barely spread). Bake 10-12 min until edges are set but centers still look soft.
Cool fully on the tray - they firm up as they cool.
Beat the yogurt, cream cheese, protein, and Splenda smooth. Add pudding mix to thicken, or chill 20-30 min. Frost the cooled cookies.

ba43d0d7-326f-4163-b2a6-5df7debb3177.name: High-Protein Anti-Pancake Sugar Cookies
ba43d0d7-326f-4163-b2a6-5df7debb3177.description: Low-flour protein sugar cookie tuned to kill the rubbery 'pancake' texture: a scoop of whey replaces most flour and SF instant pudding binds moisture for a dense bakery chew. Sweetener is granulated Splenda; pudding modeled as the Simply Delish SF vanilla already in the library; butter is regular unsalted, already halved to 7 g. Leavening is baking powder only (no soda / cream of tartar). Final step is a fridge rest. Source: user. Stated batch macros ~428 kcal / 44P / 41C / 11F (107/cookie at 4).
ba43d0d7-326f-4163-b2a6-5df7debb3177.author: user

steps.instructionText: Whisk the dry base — AP flour, whey protein, SF pudding mix, Splenda, baking powder, salt — until there are no protein clumps.
Add the melted butter, whole egg, Greek yogurt, and vanilla into the dry mix.
Stir to combine, then stop and let the bowl sit untouched 5 minutes so the whey and pudding starches swell into a shapeable dough. If still tacky, chill 5 more minutes.
Preheat oven/air fryer to 325F. Divide into 4 equal balls on parchment and press down firmly with your palm or a glass — this dough sets in the shape you give it, it won't spread.
Bake at 325F for 6-7 minutes. Pull the moment the edges look dry and set, even if the centers still look slightly soft — whey dries out fast.
Leave the cookies on the hot sheet 5-10 minutes; residual pan heat finishes the centers without making the protein rubbery.
Final step: rest in the fridge ~10 minutes to fully set, then eat.

f61a9f7b-9ba5-4ba6-8489-d802b18a27e8.name: High-Protein Nanaimo Bars
f61a9f7b-9ba5-4ba6-8489-d802b18a27e8.description: A high-protein, lower-calorie take on the classic three-layer Canadian Nanaimo bar: cocoa-coconut base, butterscotch protein filling, baking-chocolate top. Sweetener modeled as granulated Splenda; the 'or 20g oat flour' alt for coconut flour is not taken. Source: instagram. Stated full batch ~1300 kcal / 83C / 118P / 58F (header lists per-bar at 10 bars, but the directions slice 9 — yield set to 9).
f61a9f7b-9ba5-4ba6-8489-d802b18a27e8.author: user

steps.instructionText: Toast the shredded coconut ~30 seconds in a nonstick pan until golden brown.
Mix the dry base ingredients (with the toasted coconut), then add the almond milk and egg whites.
Line a baking dish with parchment and press down the base layer.
Mix the filling ingredients to a glossy frosting consistency and spread over the base.
Freeze 20 minutes.
Melt the baking chocolate and coconut oil together in 30-second intervals; sweeten the melt with liquid monk fruit to taste.
Pour and spread the chocolate over the bars.
Set in the fridge ~20 min uncovered, then refrigerate overnight covered. Slice into 9 bars.

8cb9cd00-5dae-4f76-8ff3-b95e7c3d7ad5.name: Protein Ice Cream Sandwich
8cb9cd00-5dae-4f76-8ff3-b95e7c3d7ad5.description: Healthy protein ice cream sandwiches in the style of Fat Boys: a blended vanilla protein 'ice cream' between chocolate graham crackers. Source: healthbeet.org. Decent as-is; the filling really wants a Ninja Creami churn for texture. Stated macros ~135 kcal / 16C / 13-14P / 3F per sandwich.
8cb9cd00-5dae-4f76-8ff3-b95e7c3d7ad5.author: user

steps.instructionText: Blend the protein powder, almond milk, pudding mix, Greek yogurt, vanilla, and salt smooth in a blender.
Freeze ~6 hours, until mostly frozen. Re-churn in a Ninja Creami (best) or re-blend in a food processor.
Line a cake pan with parchment and arrange half-sheets of graham cracker, 6 across the bottom.
Spread the ice cream over the crackers until all are covered.
Top with a second graham cracker and return to the freezer until frozen. Slice into 6 sandwiches.

aa6e7f25-1c7e-4abd-a363-23a9781cb8b0.name: 20g Protein Frosted Cookies
aa6e7f25-1c7e-4abd-a363-23a9781cb8b0.description: Macro-friendly frosted protein cookie (Instagram) ingested as a crumbl base-chassis candidate. Notable for a claimed <10 kcal per g protein, achieved by protein-loading: 3 scoops whey in only 5 cookies plus a full cup of nonfat Greek yogurt in the frosting. Modeled with regular AP flour (source said 'Bob's Red Mill GF flour or regular flour'); generic sweetener -> granulated Splenda; the 1 egg white authored as 33 g (one large white) since the library egg-white has no count equivalence; sprinkles ('to taste') omitted so it reads as a clean unfrosted-chassis sibling to the other crumbl cookies. Component keys aligned with the Birthday Cake base so compare row-aligns and promote can graft by key. Not yet made.
aa6e7f25-1c7e-4abd-a363-23a9781cb8b0.author: user

steps.instructionText: Mix the dry ingredients together — flour, vanilla protein, Splenda, baking powder.
Add the wet ingredients (egg, egg white, melted butter, almond milk, vanilla) to the dry and mix to a dough.
Coat your hands in flour to make the dough easier to shape; divide into 5 cookies on a lined sheet.
Bake at 350F for 10 minutes.
Allow the cookies to cool fully before frosting.
Mix the Greek yogurt, SF vanilla pudding mix, vanilla, and a dash of salt into a frosting; spread on the cooled cookies (add sprinkles to taste).

1f00b708-6c77-4cc7-991d-f481d9733ac8.name: Giant Single-Serve Protein Cookie
1f00b708-6c77-4cc7-991d-f481d9733ac8.description: Yogurt-based high-protein cookie (no butter/sugar) found on Instagram, untried. Coconut flour subbed to oat flour; sugar-free chips to semi-sweet; 'zero cal sugar' to granulated Splenda. Chocolate chips unspecified in source — assumed 20g.
1f00b708-6c77-4cc7-991d-f481d9733ac8.author: user

steps.instructionText: Mix all the ingredients together until smooth, folding in the chocolate chips last.
Divide onto parchment paper and press each into a flat round so it bakes evenly.
Bake 7-9 minutes until the edges are set but the center is still soft.
Let cool and enjoy.

Bake at 325F for ~45 min (or 350F for ~35-40 min if you want it faster) — NO water bath; a dry bake sets it denser. Pull when the center is just set with only a slight wobble (~155-160F center), not visibly jiggly. It firms further as it cools — do not chase a fully dry center, that is where a lean cheesecake turns rubbery.
Cool: oven off and door cracked 10-20 min, then counter to room temp ~1-1.5 hr (max 2 hr out), then fridge at least 4 hr — overnight is best and gives the densest, firmest set. Best the next day.

f4d2a3bd-3eb4-4dcf-b552-e7f2a4f515a8.name: Crumbl Base Protein Cookie
f4d2a3bd-3eb4-4dcf-b552-e7f2a4f515a8.description: The shared base cookie for the crumbl protein-cookie family — the flavor-neutral vanilla chassis every flavor derives from. Built from the tested-excellent set: Birthday Cake + Lemon dough at 1.5 scoops plus their shared cornstarch soft-crumb trick, with a lean egg white for structure. Frosting is a sub_recipe pin (defaults to the Cool-Whip vanilla protein frosting); flavors swap the pin — cream cheese for red velvet, a lemon drizzle for lemon. The 20g newcomer's heavier protein-load stays a queued experiment. Sweetener is granulated Splenda (calorically modeled, never monk fruit).
f4d2a3bd-3eb4-4dcf-b552-e7f2a4f515a8.author: user

steps.instructionText: Mix the dry ingredients into a bowl — flour, vanilla protein, Splenda, baking powder, salt.
Add the wet ingredients — egg, melted butter, Greek yogurt, almond milk, vanilla — and mix to a dough. If dry, add the almond milk 1 tsp at a time until it just comes together.
Chill 20-30 min, then scoop onto a lined sheet, wet hands, and flatten (this dough barely spreads).
Bake at 350F for ~9 minutes — pull while the centers still look soft; they firm up as they cool.
Cool the cookies fully, then frost with the vanilla protein frosting (sub-recipe).

65d3e83f-d3a8-4147-9aa0-67451341908e.name: Browned-Butter Protein Cookies (Soft-Chewy)
65d3e83f-d3a8-4147-9aa0-67451341908e.author: user

steps.instructionText: Brown the butter: melt it in a pan over medium heat, swirling, until golden and nutty-smelling (3-4 min). Pour into a mixing bowl and let cool a couple minutes so it won't cook the egg.
Mix wet: whisk in the brown sugar, Splenda, whole egg, egg white, and vanilla until smooth and glossy. Don't whip air in - just combine.

Add dry: stir in the whey, flour, pudding mix, baking soda, and salt until a thick, scoopable dough forms, then fold in the chocolate chips. It should hold its shape, not pour. Too stiff/dry -> a splash of milk; too loose -> a bit more flour. Taste for sweetness.
Chill the dough 30 minutes.
Shape into 3 tall mounds - do NOT flatten.
Bake at 350F / 175C for 9-11 minutes. Pull when the edges are set but the center still looks slightly underdone/soft. Stays pale - don't wait for golden tops.
Rest: leave the cookies on the hot tray 10-15 min after baking - they finish cooking from residual heat and firm up from floppy to chewy as they cool. Don't judge texture until fully cooled.
Fridge (optional): for a firmer, denser, chewier bite, refrigerate the cooled cookies 20-30 min or longer. Protein cookies tighten up when cold. Store leftovers in the fridge.

payload.instructionText: Chill only ~15 minutes (or let cold dough sit 10 min at room temp before baking). A fridge-cold center lags the most, so less chilling helps the middle keep pace.

steps.instructionText: Preheat oven to 350F. Line a tray with parchment.
Whisk the wets - yogurt, pumpkin, egg, vanilla, food coloring, vinegar - then stir in the Splenda.
Add the dries - cocoa, oat flour, cornstarch, pudding, protein, baking soda, salt. Mix to a thick dough.
Rest 10 min to hydrate. If still batter-like, add oat flour 1 tbsp at a time until scoopable.
Scoop 5 cookies, flatten slightly (they barely spread). Bake 10-12 min until edges are set but centers still look soft.
Cool fully on the tray - they firm up as they cool.
Beat the yogurt, cream cheese, protein, and Splenda smooth. Add pudding mix to thicken, or chill 20-30 min. Frost the cooled cookies.

steps.instructionText: Whisk the Greek yogurt, butterscotch pudding mix, Splenda, almond milk, and salt smooth. It should be thick and spoonable like a loose caramel — chill 10 min if you want it firmer to swirl.

27d84ce5-493c-40c6-b034-22728333629d.description: The real, glossy caramel for the top — brown sugar cooked with a little butter and thinned with almond milk, salted. Genuine caramelized-sugar flavor and drizzle (you can't caramelize Splenda), kept deliberately small: ~121 cal for the whole cake, the one honest-sugar indulgence. Sub-recipe (1 batch).
27d84ce5-493c-40c6-b034-22728333629d.author: user

steps.instructionText: Melt the butter in a small saucepan, add the brown sugar and salt, and cook over medium, stirring, until it bubbles and darkens slightly (~2-3 min). Off heat, whisk in the almond milk (it will sputter) until smooth. Cool slightly — it thickens as it sits; rewarm to drizzle.

43c3c301-7bf2-4d81-8814-d5bf461641a8.name: Turtle Dark-Chocolate Ganache
43c3c301-7bf2-4d81-8814-d5bf461641a8.description: Chocolate drizzle for the top — unsweetened baking chocolate melted with granulated Splenda and thinned with warm almond milk. Real cocoa-butter mouthfeel that cocoa powder can't fake; kept to a thin drizzle so the fat stays small (~98 cal for the whole cake). Sub-recipe (1 batch).
43c3c301-7bf2-4d81-8814-d5bf461641a8.author: user

steps.instructionText: Melt the baking chocolate gently (microwave in 15-sec bursts, or a double boiler). Whisk in the Splenda and the WARM almond milk a little at a time until glossy and pourable — adding it cold can seize the chocolate. Use it warm; it sets as it cools.

8c570673-a3d5-4ae5-adb9-d9d5e417ec68.name: Turtle Protein Cheesecake
8c570673-a3d5-4ae5-adb9-d9d5e417ec68.author: user
8c570673-a3d5-4ae5-adb9-d9d5e417ec68.commitMessage: derive variant from Vanilla Pumpkin Protein Cheesecake

steps.instructionText: Cook the pumpkin in a nonstick pan over medium heat, 3-5 min, stirring, until thickened and nutty. Remove and COOL COMPLETELY (hot mix will scramble the eggs).
Blend the cottage cheese completely smooth (immersion blender or food processor) - no visible curd, or the cheesecake turns grainy.

Beat the light cream cheese alone until smooth, then add yogurt, blended cottage cheese, protein powder, sweetener, vanilla, lemon, and vanilla pudding mix. Sift/whisk in the AP flour smooth (no lumps) and mix just until combined — don't overwork it. Warm the bowl ~15 sec if it clumps.
Add the eggs and egg white and the cooled pumpkin. Mix just until smooth - do NOT overbeat once eggs are in (air = cracks, and we want a dense set anyway).
Pour into a parchment-lined 9-inch pan. Tap on the counter to level and pop bubbles.

Bake at 325F for ~45 min (or 350F for ~35-40 min if you want it faster) — NO water bath; a dry bake sets it denser. Pull when the center is just set with only a slight wobble (~155-160F center), not visibly jiggly. It firms further as it cools — do not chase a fully dry center, that is where a lean cheesecake turns rubbery.
Cool: oven off and door cracked 10-20 min, then counter to room temp ~1-1.5 hr (max 2 hr out), then fridge at least 4 hr — overnight is best and gives the densest, firmest set. Best the next day.

payload.instructionText: Make the crust while the pumpkin cools: stir the graham crumbs, cocoa, Splenda, and salt together. Work in the melted butter and egg white until it clumps like wet sand, then press firmly into the bottom of a parchment-lined 9-inch pan — run the parchment up two opposite sides as a sling so you can lift the whole cake out later. Bake at 350F for ~10 min, then cool while you finish the filling.

Work in the melted butter and egg white until it clumps like wet sand, then press firmly into the bottom of a parchment-lined 9-inch pan — run the parchment up two opposite sides as a sling so you can lift the whole cake out later. Bake at 350F for ~10 min, then cool while you finish the filling.

Once fully chilled (ideally overnight), lift the cake out with the parchment sling onto a plate. Warm the caramel and the ganache separately just until pourable — a few seconds; they should ribbon, not run. Drizzle both over the top in a back-and-forth criss-cross (use a spoon, or a zip-top bag with a tiny corner snipped). Finish with a pinch of flaky salt. Slice with a hot, wiped knife.

Just before serving, drizzle the sugar-free maple syrup lightly over the top (or each slice) in a thin ribbon — add it last so it stays glossy and doesn't soak in. A tiny extra pinch of flaky salt over the maple plays up a salted-maple edge against the caramel and chocolate. Zero added sugar, so it costs nothing on the macros.

steps.instructionText: Cook the pumpkin in a nonstick pan over medium heat, 3-5 min, stirring, until thickened and nutty. Remove and COOL COMPLETELY (hot mix will scramble the eggs).
Blend the cottage cheese completely smooth (immersion blender or food processor) - no visible curd, or the cheesecake turns grainy.

Beat the light cream cheese alone until smooth, then add yogurt, blended cottage cheese, protein powder, sweetener, vanilla, lemon, and vanilla pudding mix. Sift/whisk in the AP flour smooth (no lumps) and mix just until combined — don't overwork it. Warm the bowl ~15 sec if it clumps.
Add the eggs and egg white and the cooled pumpkin. Mix just until smooth - do NOT overbeat once eggs are in (air = cracks, and we want a dense set anyway).

Pour half the filling onto the cooled crust. Drop spoonfuls of the protein caramel swirl over it, add the rest of the filling, then drag a knife through in a few S-strokes to marble (don't overmix). Tap the pan on the counter to level and pop bubbles.

Bake at 325F for ~45 min (or 350F for ~35-40 min if you want it faster) — NO water bath; a dry bake sets it denser. Pull when the center is just set with only a slight wobble (~155-160F center), not visibly jiggly. It firms further as it cools — do not chase a fully dry center, that is where a lean cheesecake turns rubbery.

9ea5e493-ea36-477b-9bff-691d080277ec.description: Turtle protein cheesecake derived from the Vanilla Pumpkin base — pecan skipped, so caramel + chocolate carry the turtle identity. Adds a thin graham-forward chocolate crust (graham crumbs + cocoa, egg-white bound, minimal butter), a protein caramel swirl marbled into the filling (butterscotch SF pudding + 0% yogurt), and a top drizzle of REAL brown-sugar caramel + unsweetened-baking-chocolate ganache, finished with flaky salt. Filling protein bumped 2 -> 2.5 scoops to hold the macro ratio. Lands ~11.5 cal/g protein (~217 cal / 19 g protein per slice of 8). Splenda throughout, never monk fruit; the real caramel is the one honest-sugar indulgence, kept small. Bake in a 9-inch pan with a parchment sling for lift-out.
